blocks.content: They were near the far edge of the fairgrounds when Lena appeared.

Not following — finding. Lena had the ability to locate Cassandra in a crowd without appearing to navigate, which was one of the things about a seven-year friendship that Cassandra had stopped questioning and simply relied on.

She fell into step on Cassandra's other side, and the transition was seamless. Ethan read the moment without being told — read it in the way of someone who had learned to pay attention — and said simply:

He released her hand and peeled away and Cassandra watched him go — the blue shirt moving through the crowd, his particular way of moving through a space, which she had memorized without realizing she was memorizing it.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Come sit with me for a minute.
